body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: My overall academic year experience in the Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning program at the School of Information Technology has unfortunately been inadequate and has fallen considerably short of the expectations I had when I enrolled. Persistent issues with teaching quality, administrative responsiveness, infrastructure reliability, and student support have collectively impacted my ability to learn and thrive in this environment. I feel that despite making this feedback heard through multiple channels, meaningful change has been slow and the institution's response to student concerns has lacked urgency and accountability. I sincerely hope the upcoming academic year brings substantive improvements in all the areas I have identified, not only for my own benefit but for all students who will follow in future cohorts.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: Career preparation support from the Career Center at the School of Information Technology has been disappointing and has left many students — including myself — feeling ill-equipped for the competitive graduate employment market. Career workshops are infrequent, poorly promoted, and often scheduled at times that conflict with lecture commitments, reducing student participation. The internship database is outdated and contains many positions that are no longer active, and support for students during internship placements is minimal to nonexistent. I recommend the university to substantially reinvest in the Career Center — expanding its employer network, modernizing its tools, and increasing the frequency and quality of career development programming across all academic programs and year levels.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning curriculum at the School of Information Technology covers the essential theoretical foundations competently and provides a reasonable structure for academic progression throughout the degree. Core modules are decent in their depth and breadth, covering key concepts that form the basis of professional practice in this field. However, the elective offerings could be significantly expanded to reflect emerging trends and specialized career pathways that students are increasingly interested in. Greater integration of industry collaboration — such as guest lectures, live projects, and sponsored capstone work — would substantially strengthen the curriculum's practical relevance and better prepare graduates for competitive employment.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: The extracurricular opportunities available at the School of Information Technology, coordinated by the Student Affairs Office, are remarkable and represent a genuinely important complement to the formal academic curriculum. A wide and diverse range of student clubs, cultural societies, sports teams, and volunteer programs are actively supported and well-resourced by the university, enabling students to develop leadership skills, build social connections, and pursue interests outside their academic programs. Events are well-promoted, regularly scheduled, and inclusive in their design, welcoming students from all backgrounds and programs to participate equally. I applaud the institution's recognition that holistic student development extends beyond classroom performance and into every dimension of campus life.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: Career preparation services at the School of Information Technology, offered through the Career Center, are satisfactory and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: The financial services managed by the Finance Department at the School of Information Technology have been a source of inconsistent experiences for me and several of my peers this semester, and I feel it is important to communicate these concerns formally. Scholarship disbursements were delayed by over six weeks this semester without advance notice or clear explanation to affected students, creating significant financial hardship for those who rely on these payments. Fee statements are sometimes inaccurate, requiring multiple correction requests that take considerable time and follow-up effort to resolve. I strongly urge the Finance Department to implement automated disbursement tracking, improve error-checking processes, and establish proactive communication protocols when delays are anticipated.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure and digital learning environment provided by the IT Support Department is exceptional and has greatly supported my studies at the School of Information Technology this semester. Campus Wi-Fi coverage is consistently strong and reliable across all academic buildings, laboratories, and study spaces, enabling seamless online collaboration and research activities throughout the day. Computer labs are well-equipped with up-to-date hardware and licensed software relevant to my field, reducing the burden on students to source tools independently. The IT helpdesk is responsive and knowledgeable, resolving technical issues promptly and minimizing disruption to student workflows. I acknowledge the university's ongoing investment in digital infrastructure.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: Learning resources for my Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning program at the School of Information Technology are moderate, with some courses providing excellent, well-curated materials and others offering minimal guidance beyond the core required textbook. The library's digital collection is reasonable in scope but has some notable gaps in specialized resources relevant to advanced topics in our field. The LMS functions adequately as a distribution platform, though its use varies significantly between lecturers, creating an inconsistent experience across modules. A more standardized approach to resource provision, with minimum content requirements per course, would considerably improve the consistency and quality of the learning environment.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: Academic administrative processes at the School of Information Technology are reasonable in their execution, meeting basic requirements but leaving clear room for improvement in both speed and user-friendliness. The online student portal functions adequately for routine tasks such as registration and fee payment, but navigating more complex requests — such as grade appeals or course exemptions — remains unnecessarily cumbersome. Communication timelines are inconsistent, with some requests handled promptly and others taking weeks without updates or acknowledgment. Streamlining administrative workflows, improving the student portal's UX, and setting clear service-level response standards would meaningfully reduce administrative friction for the entire student body.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning student experience at the School of Information Technology center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only reasonable outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
